response.justification: Strong technical foundation in distributed systems and high-throughput architecture, with current hands-on experience in AI-native engineering at re:cinq. However, the complete absence of Python—explicitly listed as must-have 'Strong Python'—is disqualifying unless flexible. Operational ownership and incident-response readiness are also not adequately demonstrated despite being core responsibilities.
strengths: Currently working in AI-native engineering environment building AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflows—exactly the environment and mindset the role requires

Proven distributed systems expertise at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, high-performance architecture; broad systems thinking across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains
Strong TypeScript, modern engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development), and multiple staff-level roles demonstrating technical strategy, ownership mindset, and architectural depth
concerns: No Python experience in skills or background—critical gap given explicit must-have requirement for 'Strong Python' expertise
Limited evidence of operational ownership, monitoring, incident response, or on-call readiness; background emphasizes development and architecture over operational responsibility
Agile ceremony participation (two-week sprints, scrum champion rotation) not explicitly demonstrated; career focus on trunk-based development rather than structured agile frameworks

key_factors.factor: Current AI-native role with distributed systems background
Weak operational ownership and on-call evidence
